properties.title: Turkey to race ahead of EU on battery storage amid fossil fuel crisis
properties.summary: More than 33GW of battery capacity approved for Turkish grid since 2022 compared with 12-13GW in Germany Turkey has given the green light to more batteries to buffer its electricity grid than any EU member state, a report has found, in a further sign of rich countries losing steam in the race to a clean economy. properties.title: Australia says it can’t go it alone on social media ban for kids
properties.summary: Countries should get behind a global movement to shift the norms and laws around how kids use social media, Australia’s ambassador to the EU told POLITICO. Australia in December became the first country in the world to implement restrictions on kids’ use of social media, effectively banning under-16s from using popular platforms like TikTok and YouTube in a move that has spurred similar efforts from European countries. properties.summary: As the US vice-president wades into a heated campaign, Hungary’s leader faces the real possibility of defeat Even before the plane carrying JD and Usha Vance had landed in Budapest, the Hungarian government had hailed their two-day visit as a new golden age in the relationship between Washington and Budapest. properties.title: Why Hungary’s energy policy is less MAGA than JD Vance might think

properties.summary: Orbán offered his support to Putin, likening his assistance to that of a mouse helping a lion in a well-known Hungarian fable, according to a phone conversation transcript obtained by news agency Bloomberg.

properties.title: Gunmen open fire near Israeli consulate building in Istanbul
properties.summary: Three gunmen were involved in a shootout with police near the Israeli consulate in Istanbul on Tuesday, leaving one attacker dead and two wounded and in custody, Turkish authorities said. One of the attackers had links to an “organization that exploits religion,” said Turkish Minister of the Interior Mustafa Çiftçi in a statement . properties.title: JD Vance slams Brussels ‘bureaucrats’ for meddling in Hungary before election
properties.summary: BUDAPEST — U.S. Vice President JD Vance on Tuesday insisted that Brussels rather than Washington was interfering in Hungarian politics before a crunch election on April 12, which will decide the future of key MAGA ally Prime Minister Viktor Orbán. properties.title: Centauros: Greece's anti-drone system that changes 'war of drones' at low cost
properties.summary: The anti-drone system, named after a mythical creature symbolising untamed forces of nature, uses electronic warfare technology to detect and neutralise UAVs without munitions.

properties.title: Orbán’s rival Magyar slams JD Vance’s Hungary visit as election meddling
properties.summary: Hungarian opposition leader Péter Magyar on Tuesday accused the U.S. of meddling in the country’s upcoming election, hitting out at Vice President JD Vance’s visit to Budapest just days before voters head to the polls. “No foreign country may interfere in Hungarian elections. properties.title: Hungarian Easter tradition sees women doused with water in Hollókő
properties.summary: A centuries-old Easter tradition continues in a Hungarian village, where men pour water over women in a ritual rooted in folklore and springtime customs.

properties.title: EU foreign policy shambles triggers calls for radical overhaul of diplomacy
properties.summary: BRUSSELS ― Europe’s mounting foreign policy failures, from struggling to fund Ukraine to its fragmented response to the Iran war, are fueling calls for a root-and-branch overhaul of how the bloc conducts diplomacy. properties.title: Greek PM vows to tackle ‘deep state’ in wake of farm fraud scandal
properties.summary: Kyriakos Mitsotakis calls alleged scamming of EU agricultural funds ‘a turning point’ The Greek prime minister has vowed to tackle what he has called a “deep state” he says is plaguing the country, as he sought to address a growing political crisis over a farm fraud scandal that has forced the resignation of multiple government ministers. properties.title: Les coulisses de la victoire de Lille pour décrocher l’Autorité douanière européenne
properties.summary: PARIS — En se rendant à son bureau dans la banlieue parisienne par un mercredi matin orageux, Yann Favennec, 47 ans, fonctionnaire des douanes, s’attendait à une journée bonne, mais stressante. properties.title: Serbia looks for pipeline sabotage plot culprit as Vance's Hungary visit nears
properties.summary: Serbia has opened a probe into the attempted pipeline sabotage after explosives were found near Kanjiža, as military intelligence suspects a migrant with training, raising the temperature before a key election in Hungary on Sunday.

properties.title: Mitsotakis urges swift EU probe into MPs over Greek farm subsidy fraud
properties.summary: Greek PM Kyriakos Mitsotakis has asked EU prosecutors to swiftly charge MPs over alleged €23m farm subsidy fraud, as resignations and pressure mount.

properties.title: Hungary’s Orbán is selling Christian nationalism. His former pastor isn’t buying it.
properties.summary: BUDAPEST — Gábor Iványi performed Viktor Orbán’s wedding and later baptized two of his children in the 1990s, little suspecting his friend’s government would one day put him on trial. Now, the prime minister is seeking to extend his 16-year rule over Hungary in an election on April 12, casting himself as the candidate who will protect traditional Christian values.
